tools.name: search_businesses
tools.description: Search for service businesses by location. Returns businesses with their booking URLs, ratings, and descriptions. Use get_business to see services and get_availability to check open slots.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "get_business" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Get full details for a business including services (with IDs and prices), staff, and hours. You need service_id and optionally staff_id before calling book_appo…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "create_account" references sensitive artifacts (password). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get a BookingMaven signup link pre-filled with the customer's details. The customer visits the link to set their password and activate their account, which link…

description: Get a link for a business owner to sign up and list their business on BookingMaven. Returns a URL with optional pre-filled details.

labels.name: list_my_bookings
labels.description: List all appointments booked with your email address. Requires signing in (OAuth) — your AI must be connected with an authenticated account that has a verified email. Returns your upcoming and past bookings.

labels.name: link_account
labels.description: Link the guest bookings you made with your email to your signed-in account so you can manage them from your account. Requires signing in (OAuth) with a verified email.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "get_business" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Get full details for a business including services (with IDs and prices), staff, and hours. You need service_id and optionally staff_id before calling book_appo…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "create_account" references sensitive artifacts (password). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get a BookingMaven signup link pre-filled with the customer's details. The customer visits the link to set their password and activate their account, which link…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search_businesses, start_business_signup, list_my_bookings · Private data: create_account · Public sink: list_my_bookings, link_account.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search_businesses, start_business_signup, list_my_bookings · Private data: create_account · Public sink: list_my_bookings, link_account

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
